rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Runtime-fork prompt blocks: No dual-runtime forks in Cowork-only training.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line:88 — `bosser-strategy:content-strategy-claude-basics.md` pseudo-URI used correctly in maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cowork is sandboxed to its connected working directories.
Cowork capability surface — no session rename in Cowork or Desktop.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:7 — `**Session** *(new, "Organisers rollout synthesis")*` in a Cowork-only exercise. Per rule 25, Cowork has no rename API; task names must be set at New-task creation. The session widget renderer auto-wraps `/rename` `<pre>` blocks in `.rt-cli` — meaning the name instruction renders only for CLI users. In a Cowork-only context, this widget may produce a chip the student cannot act on, or silently not render.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Verify whether the session widget renders usefully in Cowork (task name set at creation, not via /rename). If not, replace with a plain instruction naming what to call the Cowork task at creation time.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line:73 — `Let's focus on:` is the last line of Prompt 3; this is an open-hook pattern (colon invites student input), not a bracket placeholder. Student input is genuinely required (they name which point to draft). Open-hook is valid per rule 1 qualifier.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:62-63 — Lead-in before Prompt 3 is conditional: "If the first answer gives you the right angle but not the words yet, ask Claude to draft the point in plain organiser language." Rule 2 requires an imperative command-verb lead-in under 20 words. This is a conditional/explanatory sentence, not an action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rewrite as imperative: "Ask Claude to draft the share-back point in plain organiser language." Move the conditional context to surrounding body prose.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Claude Basics is Cowork-only; no dual-runtime forks required per training CLAUDE.md.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Preamble before action — design around it.
Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.

platform_claims_checked.claim: **Session** *(new, "Organisers rollout synthesis")* — session widget implies naming a Cowork task via rename
All three prompts labeled *(Cowork, organisers only)* — runtime claim that Cowork supports reading shared/ folder files and updating chat readout
"Continuously read the group rollout synthesis files in `shared/`" and "After each update, wait briefly, check `shared/` again" — continuous autonomous polling loop in Cowork
Save the latest version as `shared/organisers-rollout-readout.md` — Cowork can write to the connected shared/ folder
No dual-runtime fork divs used — consistent with Cowork-only training architecture
